evidence_summary: Graph neural networks can utilize graph structure to enhance node classification accuracy by encoding the graph structure directly using a neural network model.
Semi-supervised learning on graph-structured data can be achieved through an efficient variant of convolutional neural networks that operate directly on graphs.
The assumption that connected nodes in the graph are likely to share the same label may restrict modeling capacity, as graph edges need not necessarily encode node similarity.
